seoTitle: IXYS Products | Power MOSFETs, IGBTs, Diodes, Thyristors | LiTong Distributor
seoDescription: Explore IXYS product portfolio including power MOSFETs, IGBT modules, fast recovery diodes, and thyristors. Authorized distributor with technical support and selection guidance.

faqs.question: What are the main product categories offered by IXYS?
faqs.answer: IXYS offers four main power semiconductor product categories: (1) Power MOSFETs - high-voltage and low-voltage MOSFETs for switching applications with industry-leading on-resistance; (2) IGBT Modules - insulated gate bipolar transistors for medium to high power motor drives and power supplies; (3) Power Diodes - fast recovery and standard recovery diodes for rectification, freewheeling, and snubber applications; (4) Thyristors - silicon controlled rectifiers (SCRs) and triacs for phase control and AC power switching. Each category features multiple series optimized for specific voltage, current, and switching requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Review each product category to find the IXYS solution matching your power electronics requirements.

faqs.question: How do I select the right IXYS power semiconductor for my application?
faqs.answer: Selecting the right IXYS power semiconductor involves several key considerations: (1) Voltage rating - choose Vds or Vce at least 20-30% higher than maximum operating voltage; (2) Current rating - select continuous current rating based on thermal constraints and peak current requirements; (3) Switching frequency - for high-frequency applications (>100kHz), consider MOSFETs; for lower frequencies, IGBTs may be more efficient; (4) Thermal requirements - evaluate Rth and maximum junction temperature for your cooling solution; (5) Package - select TO-220, TO-247, D2PAK, or modules based on power dissipation and mounting requirements. Contact LiTong FAE for detailed selection guidance and thermal modeling.
faqs.decisionGuide: Match voltage/current ratings to your application with adequate margins. Contact FAE for thermal analysis and selection support.

faqs.question: What are the key advantages of IXYS power MOSFETs?
faqs.answer: IXYS power MOSFETs offer several competitive advantages: (1) Low on-resistance (Rds(on)) - enabling lower conduction losses and higher efficiency; (2) Fast switching speed - reducing switching losses in high-frequency applications; (3) High voltage capability - products available from 40V to 1000V+ for various applications; (4) Robust avalanche capability - many devices rated for unclamped inductive switching; (5) Excellent thermal performance - low thermal resistance packages for effective heat dissipation; (6) Wide product range - from small-signal to high-power devices in various packages. IXYS MOSFETs are particularly strong in high-voltage applications above 500V.
faqs.decisionGuide: IXYS MOSFETs are ideal for high-voltage, high-frequency switching applications requiring low losses and reliable performance.

faqs.question: How do IXYS IGBT modules compare to discrete IGBTs?
faqs.answer: IXYS IGBT modules offer significant advantages over discrete IGBTs for higher power applications: (1) Integrated design - modules include IGBT chips, freewheeling diodes, and thermal management in one package; (2) Higher power density - modules can handle hundreds of amps in compact packages; (3) Better thermal performance - direct bond copper (DBC) substrate provides excellent heat spreading; (4) Simplified assembly - reduces component count and assembly time; (5) Improved reliability - factory-tested modules ensure consistent performance. IXYS offers modules in various configurations including half-bridge, full-bridge, and three-phase topologies for motor drives, inverters, and power supplies.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose IGBT modules for applications above 50A; discrete IGBTs are suitable for lower current applications.

faqs.question: What makes IXYS fast recovery diodes suitable for high-frequency applications?
faqs.answer: IXYS fast recovery diodes are optimized for high-frequency power electronics with: (1) Fast reverse recovery time (trr) - typically 25-100ns for minimal switching losses; (2) Low reverse recovery charge (Qrr) - reducing EMI and switching stress on associated devices; (3) Soft recovery characteristics - minimizing voltage overshoots and ringing; (4) High surge current capability - handling inrush currents in power supplies; (5) Wide voltage range - available from 100V to 2000V+ for various applications. These characteristics make IXYS diodes ideal for output rectification in SMPS, freewheeling in motor drives, and snubber applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: IXYS fast recovery diodes are ideal for high-frequency switching applications requiring minimal reverse recovery losses.

categories.name: Power MOSFETs
categories.description: IXYS power MOSFETs offer industry-leading performance for switching applications with low on-resistance and fast switching characteristics.
categories.longDescription: IXYS power MOSFETs deliver exceptional performance for power conversion and switching applications. Featuring advanced trench and planar technologies, these MOSFETs offer ultra-low on-resistance, fast switching speeds, and robust avalanche capability. As an authorized IXYS distributor, LiTong provides comprehensive selection guidance for power MOSFETs ranging from 40V to 1000V+. The portfolio includes devices optimized for high-frequency SMPS, motor drives, DC-DC converters, and automotive applications. IXYS MOSFETs are available in industry-standard packages including TO-220, TO-247, D2PAK, and surface-mount options.

selectionGuide.title: IXYS Power MOSFET Selection Guide
selectionGuide.description: Comprehensive guide to selecting the right IXYS MOSFET for your power electronics application

series.name: TrenchFET Series
series.description: Low-voltage MOSFETs with ultra-low Rds(on) for high-current applications

faqs.question: What is the difference between trench and planar MOSFET technologies?
faqs.answer: Trench and planar MOSFETs differ in structure and performance characteristics: (1) Trench MOSFETs - vertical channel structure provides higher cell density, lower Rds(on), and better for low-voltage (<200V) applications; (2) Planar MOSFETs - horizontal channel structure offers better high-voltage capability (>500V) and avalanche ruggedness. IXYS offers both technologies: TrenchFET series for low-voltage high-current applications, X-Class series for high-voltage applications. Trench devices typically have lower gate charge for faster switching, while planar devices offer superior body diode performance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose trench MOSFETs for low-voltage high-current; planar MOSFETs for high-voltage applications.

faqs.question: How do I calculate power dissipation in a MOSFET?
faqs.answer: MOSFET power dissipation consists of conduction and switching losses: (1) Conduction loss - Pcond = I² × Rds(on) × duty cycle. Use Rds(on) at actual operating temperature (typically 1.5-2x 25°C value); (2) Switching loss - Psw = 0.5 × V × I × (tr + tf) × fsw, where tr/tf are rise/fall times, fsw is switching frequency; (3) Gate drive loss - Pgate = Qg × Vgs × fsw; (4) Total loss - Ptotal = Pcond + Psw + Pgate. Ensure total dissipation is within thermal limits: Tj = Ta + (Ptotal × Rth(j-a)). IXYS datasheets provide thermal and switching characteristics for accurate calculations.
faqs.decisionGuide: Calculate both conduction and switching losses at worst-case conditions to ensure reliable operation.

faqs.question: What is avalanche rating and why is it important?
faqs.answer: Avalanche rating (EAS - Single Pulse Avalanche Energy) indicates a MOSFET's ability to withstand unclamped inductive switching events: (1) Definition - energy the device can absorb when switching off an inductive load without external clamping; (2) Importance - protects against voltage transients in motor drives, solenoids, and relay applications; (3) Rating - specified in millijoules (mJ) at specific current and starting temperature; (4) IXYS advantage - many IXYS MOSFETs feature robust avalanche capability, eliminating need for external protection components. Always ensure EAS rating exceeds worst-case energy in your application.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select MOSFETs with adequate avalanche rating for inductive load applications to eliminate external protection.

faqs.question: How does temperature affect MOSFET performance?
faqs.answer: Temperature significantly impacts MOSFET characteristics: (1) Rds(on) increases with temperature - typically 0.3-0.5% per °C, meaning 50-80% higher at 125°C vs 25°C; (2) Gate threshold voltage decreases - approximately -7mV/°C; (3) Switching speed may decrease slightly at high temperatures; (4) Maximum current decreases due to higher thermal resistance. Design considerations: (1) Size for worst-case Rds(on) at maximum junction temperature; (2) Ensure adequate heatsinking for continuous operation; (3) Consider derating for high ambient temperatures. IXYS datasheets provide normalized curves for temperature effects.
faqs.decisionGuide: Always design using maximum Rds(on) at worst-case junction temperature, not the 25°C datasheet value.

faqs.question: What gate drive considerations are important for IXYS MOSFETs?
faqs.answer: Proper gate drive is critical for MOSFET performance: (1) Drive voltage - use 10-15V for standard MOSFETs, 5V for logic-level devices; (2) Gate resistor - controls switching speed and EMI; typical values 5-50Ω depending on switching frequency; (3) Drive current - must charge/discharge gate capacitance quickly; Ig = Qg/t, where t is desired switching time; (4) Miller plateau - ensure drive can supply plateau current for fast switching; (5) Negative voltage - consider -3 to -5V for high-power applications to prevent false turn-on. IXYS provides recommended gate drive circuits in application notes.
faqs.decisionGuide: Design gate drive with adequate voltage, current capability, and proper gate resistance for your switching frequency.

products.name: 600V 40A Power MOSFET
products.shortDescription: High-voltage N-channel MOSFET with 600V rating and ultra-low 85mΩ on-resistance for power supplies and motor drives.
descriptionParagraphs: The IXFH40N60P3 is a high-performance 600V N-channel power MOSFET featuring advanced planar technology. With ultra-low 85mΩ maximum on-resistance and 40A continuous current capability, it delivers excellent efficiency for power conversion applications.

This MOSFET features robust avalanche capability with 500mJ single-pulse avalanche energy rating, eliminating the need for external protection in many inductive load applications. The TO-247 package provides excellent thermal performance with low junction-to-case thermal resistance.
Ideal for high-power SMPS, motor drives, inverters, and power factor correction circuits where high efficiency and reliability are critical requirements.
features: Fast switching characteristics reduce switching losses
Low gate charge for efficient gate drive
TO-247 package with excellent thermal performance
Avalanche rated for unclamped inductive switching

faeReview.author: Michael Chen
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Power Electronics
faeReview.content: The IXFH40N60P3 is my go-to recommendation for 600V applications requiring 30-40A capability. In my experience designing industrial motor drives, this MOSFET delivers excellent thermal performance and reliability. The 85mΩ Rds(on) is competitive with newer superjunction devices, while the planar technology offers superior avalanche ruggedness. I particularly appreciate the consistent performance across temperature - the Rds(on) stays well-controlled even at high junction temperatures. For applications with inductive loads, the 500mJ avalanche rating often eliminates the need for external protection circuits, simplifying design and reducing BOM cost. I recommend using 15V gate drive for fastest switching and lowest losses.
faeReview.highlight: Excellent 600V MOSFET with robust avalanche capability for industrial motor drives and power supplies

alternativeParts.reason: Lower current rating for reduced power applications

companionParts.description: Fast recovery diode for freewheeling applications

faqs.question: What is the maximum operating frequency for this MOSFET?
faqs.answer: The IXFH40N60P3 can operate at switching frequencies up to 500kHz in hard-switching applications, and up to 1MHz in resonant or soft-switching topologies. At 100kHz switching frequency with 300V bus voltage and 20A current, switching losses are approximately 15-20W. For high-frequency operation above 200kHz, consider using resonant topologies or zero-voltage switching to minimize switching losses. The gate charge of 110nC requires adequate gate drive current - recommend using gate drivers with 2A+ source/sink capability for fast switching.
faqs.decisionGuide: Suitable for applications up to 500kHz; use soft-switching for frequencies above 200kHz.

faqs.question: What heatsink is required for 30A continuous operation?
faqs.answer: For 30A continuous operation with the IXFH40N60P3: (1) Conduction loss at 30A = 30² × 0.085 = 76.5W (using max Rds(on)); (2) Allowing for switching losses of ~10W at moderate frequencies, total dissipation ~85-90W; (3) Maximum junction temperature 150°C, ambient 40°C, allowable rise 110°C; (4) Required thermal resistance Rth(sa) = 110°C / 90W = 1.22°C/W or better. Recommended heatsinks: Profile heatsinks with forced air cooling (2-3 m/s), or extruded heatsinks with natural convection for lower currents. Always use thermal interface material with 0.1-0.3°C·cm²/W thermal resistance. Monitor case temperature to verify thermal design.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use heatsink with Rth(sa) < 1.2°C/W with forced air for 30A continuous operation.

faqs.question: Can this MOSFET be used in bridge configurations?
faqs.answer: Yes, the IXFH40N60P3 is well-suited for bridge configurations including half-bridge, full-bridge, and three-phase bridges. Key considerations: (1) Body diode - the integral body diode has typical 250ns reverse recovery time; add external fast recovery diodes in parallel for high-frequency applications; (2) Dead time - implement 200-500ns dead time to prevent shoot-through; (3) Gate drive - use isolated gate drivers for high-side switches with proper bootstrap or isolated power supplies; (4) Layout - minimize loop inductance between bridge legs to reduce voltage overshoots. For motor drive applications up to 5kW, this MOSFET provides excellent performance and reliability.
faqs.decisionGuide: Excellent for bridge topologies; add external freewheeling diodes for high-frequency applications.

faqs.question: What is the recommended gate drive voltage?
faqs.answer: The recommended gate drive voltage for IXFH40N60P3 is 10-15V for optimal performance: (1) 10V drive - provides good conduction with Rds(on) near datasheet values; (2) 12V drive - recommended for most applications, provides margin against drive voltage variations; (3) 15V drive - optimal for lowest Rds(on) and fastest switching, but increases gate drive losses; (4) Below 8V - not recommended as Rds(on) increases significantly. The gate threshold voltage is 3-5V, so 10V+ provides adequate margin. For high-reliability applications, consider using 12-15V drive with undervoltage lockout protection on the gate driver.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use 12-15V gate drive for optimal performance; never operate below 8V gate voltage.

faqs.question: How does this MOSFET compare to superjunction devices?
faqs.answer: Compared to superjunction MOSFETs, the IXFH40N60P3 planar technology offers different trade-offs: (1) Rds(on) - superjunction devices have lower Rds(on) per die area (e.g., 60mΩ vs 85mΩ for similar price); (2) Switching - superjunction devices typically have higher dV/dt and may require snubbers; (3) Avalanche - planar devices like IXFH40N60P3 have superior avalanche ruggedness; (4) EMI - planar devices often produce less EMI due to softer switching characteristics; (5) Cost - planar devices are typically more cost-effective. For applications requiring avalanche capability or with inductive loads, the IXFH40N60P3 is often the better choice despite slightly higher Rds(on).
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose IXFH40N60P3 for avalanche ruggedness; consider superjunction for lowest Rds(on) in non-inductive applications.

products.name: 150V 96A Trench MOSFET
products.shortDescription: Low-voltage N-channel trench MOSFET with 150V rating and ultra-low 6.5mΩ on-resistance for high-current DC-DC converters.
descriptionParagraphs: The IXTP96N15T is a high-performance 150V N-channel trench MOSFET designed for high-current applications. Featuring ultra-low 6.5mΩ maximum on-resistance and 96A continuous current capability, it delivers exceptional efficiency for DC-DC conversion.

Advanced trench technology enables high cell density and low gate charge (Qg = 155nC), allowing fast switching with reduced gate drive losses. The TO-220 package provides cost-effective thermal management for medium power applications.
Ideal for synchronous rectification in server power supplies, battery chargers, and high-current DC-DC converters where low conduction losses are critical.
features: High 96A current capability for high-power applications
Trench technology for fast switching performance
Low gate charge reduces gate drive requirements

faeReview.author: David Liu
faeReview.title: Principal FAE - Power Systems
faeReview.content: The IXTP96N15T is an excellent choice for synchronous rectification in high-current applications. I've used this MOSFET in multiple 48V server power supply designs with outstanding results. The 6.5mΩ Rds(on) is very competitive, and the trench technology provides excellent switching performance. At 60A continuous current, conduction losses are only about 23W - significantly lower than competing devices. The TO-220 package is cost-effective and easy to heatsink. One tip: use a good thermal interface material and ensure adequate airflow - at high currents, every degree of temperature reduction helps. For synchronous rectification, pair with a fast gate driver to minimize body diode conduction time.
faeReview.highlight: Excellent low-voltage MOSFET for high-current synchronous rectification applications

alternativeParts.reason: Lower current rating for reduced power applications
Higher voltage and current rating with lower Rds(on)
alternativeParts.useCase: Applications requiring higher voltage margin or current

companionParts.description: Matching MOSFET for synchronous rectifier pair
Fast recovery diode for output rectification

faqs.question: What is the optimal application for this MOSFET?
faqs.answer: The IXTP96N15T is optimized for low-voltage high-current applications, particularly synchronous rectification in DC-DC converters and SMPS. Its ultra-low 6.5mΩ Rds(on) minimizes conduction losses at high currents, making it ideal for: (1) 48V server power supplies - excellent for 12V output synchronous rectifiers; (2) Battery chargers - high efficiency at high charging currents; (3) Telecom rectifiers - 48V to intermediate bus conversion; (4) Motor drives - low-voltage high-current motor control. The 150V rating provides adequate margin for 48V systems with transients. For applications above 75A continuous, ensure adequate heatsinking as conduction losses become significant.
faqs.decisionGuide: Best for synchronous rectification in 48V systems and high-current DC-DC converters.

faqs.question: How do I minimize switching losses in high-frequency applications?
faqs.answer: To minimize switching losses with IXTP96N15T at high frequencies: (1) Gate drive - use low-impedance gate driver (2A+ capability) with proper gate resistor (5-10Ω); (2) Drive voltage - use 10-12V gate drive for optimal switching speed; (3) Layout - minimize gate loop inductance (<10nH) and drain-source loop inductance; (4) Snubbers - consider RC snubbers if voltage overshoot exceeds 80% of Vds rating; (5) Dead time - in bridge configurations, minimize dead time to reduce body diode conduction. At 100kHz, switching losses are typically 10-15W. For frequencies above 200kHz, consider using resonant topologies or advanced soft-switching techniques to minimize switching losses.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use strong gate drive and optimize layout for minimal switching losses at high frequencies.

faqs.question: What is the maximum power dissipation without a heatsink?
faqs.answer: Without a heatsink, the IXTP96N15T in TO-220 package has limited power handling: (1) Thermal resistance junction-to-air Rth(j-a) is approximately 62°C/W for free air mounting; (2) Maximum junction temperature is 175°C; (3) At 25°C ambient, maximum power dissipation = (175-25)/62 = 2.4W; (4) At 50°C ambient, maximum power = (175-50)/62 = 2.0W. This limits continuous current to approximately 19A at 25°C ambient (P = I² × Rds(on) = 19² × 0.0065 = 2.35W). For higher currents, a heatsink is mandatory. Even a small heatsink (Rth = 20°C/W) increases capability to 6W, supporting 30A continuous operation.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use heatsink for currents above 15A; free-air operation limited to ~2W dissipation.

faqs.question: Can this MOSFET be paralleled for higher current?
faqs.answer: Yes, the IXTP96N15T can be paralleled for higher current applications: (1) Current sharing - use individual gate resistors (1-2Ω) for each MOSFET to prevent oscillations; (2) Layout - ensure symmetric layout with equal trace lengths and impedances to each device; (3) Matching - devices from same production batch typically match within 10% for Rds(on); (4) Temperature - thermal coupling helps balance currents as Rds(on) increases with temperature; (5) Gate drive - use single gate driver with sufficient current capability (4A+ for two devices). Two IXTP96N15T in parallel can handle 150-170A continuous with proper heatsinking. Always verify current sharing with current probes during prototype testing.
faqs.decisionGuide: Can be paralleled with proper gate resistors and symmetric layout for 150A+ applications.

faqs.question: What are the key layout considerations for this MOSFET?
faqs.answer: Proper PCB layout is critical for IXTP96N15T performance: (1) Gate drive loop - minimize loop area between gate driver and MOSFET gate/source; keep traces short and wide; (2) Power loops - minimize inductance in high di/dt paths; use copper planes for drain and source connections; (3) Kelvin connection - use separate source connection for gate drive return to avoid source inductance feedback; (4) Decoupling - place ceramic capacitors (100nF-1µF) close to drain and source terminals; (5) Thermal vias - use thermal vias under the drain pad to spread heat to inner layers. Poor layout can cause voltage overshoots, oscillations, and increased switching losses. Follow IXYS application notes for recommended layouts.
faqs.decisionGuide: Minimize gate and power loop inductance; use Kelvin source connection and adequate thermal vias.

products.name: 200V 50A Power MOSFET
products.shortDescription: High-voltage N-channel MOSFET with 200V rating and low 45mΩ on-resistance for industrial power supplies.
descriptionParagraphs: The IXFH50N20 is a robust 200V N-channel power MOSFET designed for industrial power supply applications. With low 45mΩ maximum on-resistance and 50A continuous current capability, it provides reliable performance for medium-voltage power conversion.

This MOSFET features enhanced ruggedness for industrial environments with excellent avalanche capability. The TO-247 package ensures superior thermal management for continuous operation in demanding conditions.
Ideal for industrial SMPS, motor drives, battery chargers, and DC-DC converters requiring 200V+ voltage rating with moderate current capability.
features: Low 45mΩ on-resistance for reduced conduction losses
TO-247 package with excellent thermal performance

faeReview.author: Steven Park
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Industrial Power
faeReview.content: The IXFH50N20 is a solid choice for 200V industrial applications requiring 30-50A capability. I've successfully used this MOSFET in numerous industrial power supply designs. The 45mΩ Rds(on) provides good efficiency, and the 200V rating offers adequate margin for 48V and 110V industrial systems. The avalanche capability is particularly valuable in industrial environments where voltage transients are common. I recommend this device for applications requiring higher voltage margin than 150V devices can provide. Use proper gate drive (12-15V) and ensure adequate heatsinking for continuous operation above 30A.
faeReview.highlight: Reliable 200V MOSFET for industrial power supplies with good avalanche ruggedness

alternativeParts.reason: Lower voltage but much higher current and lower Rds(on)
alternativeParts.useCase: Applications not requiring 200V rating but needing higher current

alternativeParts.reason: Higher current rating with lower Rds(on)

faqs.question: What applications benefit most from the 200V rating?
faqs.answer: The 200V rating of IXFH50N20 is ideal for: (1) 48V industrial systems with high voltage transients - provides 4x voltage margin; (2) 110V DC bus applications - common in industrial equipment; (3) Battery-powered systems with 96V nominal voltage; (4) Motor drives with 90V DC bus; (5) Power supplies with 150V rectified input. The higher voltage rating provides better reliability margin compared to 150V devices in industrial environments where voltage spikes are common. For 24V and 48V systems without significant transients, lower voltage devices may offer better Rds(on) performance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose for 48V+ industrial systems or applications with significant voltage transients.

faqs.question: How does this compare to the IXTP96N15T for 48V applications?
faqs.answer: Comparing IXFH50N20 (200V) vs IXTP96N15T (150V) for 48V applications: (1) Voltage margin - IXFH50N20 provides 4x margin vs 3x for IXTP96N15T; (2) Current capability - IXTP96N15T handles 96A vs 50A for IXFH50N20; (3) Rds(on) - IXTP96N15T has 6.5mΩ vs 45mΩ for IXFH50N20 (much lower conduction losses); (4) Cost - IXTP96N15T typically more cost-effective per amp. For 48V applications without extreme transients, IXTP96N15T is usually the better choice due to much lower Rds(on). Choose IXFH50N20 only if you need the extra voltage margin for reliability in harsh industrial environments.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use IXTP96N15T for most 48V applications; IXFH50N20 only if extra voltage margin is required.

products.name: 1000V 24A Power MOSFET
products.shortDescription: Ultra-high-voltage N-channel MOSFET with 1000V rating and 385mΩ on-resistance for high-voltage power supplies.
descriptionParagraphs: The IXFH24N100 is an ultra-high-voltage 1000V N-channel power MOSFET designed for high-voltage power conversion applications. With 385mΩ maximum on-resistance and 24A continuous current capability, it provides reliable switching for high-voltage systems.

This MOSFET features IXYS's advanced planar technology optimized for high-voltage operation with excellent avalanche ruggedness. The TO-247 package provides robust thermal management for high-voltage applications.
Ideal for offline power supplies, PFC circuits, solar inverters, and high-voltage DC-DC converters requiring 1000V+ voltage capability.
features: Advanced planar technology for high-voltage operation
Low gate charge for efficient drive
TO-247 package for reliable thermal management

faeReview.author: Michael Chen
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Power Electronics
faeReview.content: The IXFH24N100 is an excellent choice for high-voltage applications requiring 1000V capability. I've used this MOSFET in PFC circuits and offline power supplies with excellent results. The 1000V rating provides adequate margin for universal input (85-265VAC) applications with PFC. While the Rds(on) of 385mΩ seems high compared to lower voltage devices, it's actually very competitive for 1000V rating. The avalanche capability is particularly important in high-voltage applications where energy stored in inductances is significant. For PFC applications, this device can handle 500-600W with proper heatsinking. I recommend using 15V gate drive and proper snubbers to minimize switching losses.
faeReview.highlight: Excellent 1000V MOSFET for high-voltage power supplies and PFC applications

alternativeParts.reason: Lower voltage but much better Rds(on) and current capability
Higher voltage rating for extreme applications

companionParts.description: Fast recovery diode for PFC applications

faqs.question: What applications require 1000V MOSFETs?
faqs.answer: 1000V MOSFETs like IXFH24N100 are required for: (1) Universal input offline power supplies (85-265VAC) with active PFC - PFC output is typically 380-400VDC, requiring 1000V devices for switching; (2) Three-phase 480VAC input systems - rectified voltage is 680VDC; (3) Solar inverters with high DC link voltages; (4) High-voltage DC-DC converters; (5) Industrial motor drives with 600V+ DC bus. The 1000V rating provides necessary margin for voltage spikes and transients in these high-voltage applications. For lower voltage systems (single-phase 230VAC or 48V DC), lower voltage MOSFETs with better Rds(on) are more appropriate.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use for universal input PFC, three-phase systems, and high-voltage DC applications.

faqs.question: How do I minimize switching losses in high-voltage applications?
faqs.answer: To minimize switching losses with IXFH24N100 in high-voltage applications: (1) Gate drive - use strong gate driver (2A+) with optimized gate resistor (10-22Ω); (2) Snubbers - use RC snubbers to reduce voltage overshoot and dV/dt; (3) Layout - minimize loop inductance to reduce ringing; (4) Soft switching - use resonant or quasi-resonant topologies if possible; (5) Switching frequency - operate at moderate frequencies (50-100kHz) to balance losses with magnetics size. At 400V bus voltage, switching losses can be significant - proper snubber design is critical. The relatively low gate charge (63nC) helps minimize gate drive losses.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use strong gate drive, proper snubbers, and consider soft-switching topologies for high-voltage applications.

categories.name: IGBT Modules
categories.description: IXYS IGBT modules combine insulated gate bipolar transistors with freewheeling diodes in compact packages for medium to high power applications.
categories.longDescription: IXYS IGBT modules provide integrated power solutions for motor drives, inverters, and power supplies requiring 50A to 600A current capability. These modules feature direct bond copper (DBC) substrate technology for excellent thermal performance and reliability. Available in half-bridge, full-bridge, and three-phase configurations, IXYS IGBT modules simplify design and reduce assembly time. The portfolio includes 600V, 1200V, and 1700V modules optimized for industrial motor drives, renewable energy systems, and UPS applications. Integrated NTC temperature sensors and low-inductance design ensure safe, efficient operation.

selectionGuide.title: IXYS IGBT Module Selection Guide
selectionGuide.description: Comprehensive guide to selecting the right IXYS IGBT module for your power electronics application

series.name: M-Series
series.description: Standard IGBT modules for general industrial applications

series.name: S-Series
series.description: High-speed IGBT modules for high-frequency applications

faqs.question: What are the advantages of IGBT modules over discrete IGBTs?
faqs.answer: IGBT modules offer several advantages: (1) Integrated design - combines IGBT chips, freewheeling diodes, and thermal management in one package; (2) Higher power density - modules can handle 100-600A in compact packages; (3) Better thermal performance - DBC substrate provides excellent heat spreading and low thermal resistance; (4) Simplified assembly - reduces component count and assembly time; (5) Improved reliability - factory-tested with consistent performance; (6) Built-in features - many modules include NTC temperature sensors and low-inductance design. Modules are ideal for applications above 50A where discrete devices would require paralleling.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose modules for applications above 50A or when simplified assembly is desired.

faqs.question: How do I select the right IGBT module voltage rating?
faqs.answer: IGBT module voltage selection guidelines: (1) 600V modules - suitable for 220-240V AC applications including single-phase drives and appliances; (2) 1200V modules - most common for 380-480V AC industrial drives and inverters; (3) 1700V modules - for high-voltage applications including 690V AC drives and traction systems. Select Vces rating at least 2-3x the DC bus voltage to account for voltage spikes. For 380V AC systems with PFC (680V DC bus), use 1200V modules. Always include safety margin for voltage transients.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use 600V for 230V systems, 1200V for 380-480V systems, 1700V for high-voltage applications.

products.name: 1200V 60A IGBT Module
products.shortDescription: Dual IGBT module with 1200V rating and 60A continuous current for motor drives and inverters.
descriptionParagraphs: The MIXA60PD1200TS is a dual IGBT module featuring two 1200V 60A IGBTs with integrated freewheeling diodes. Built on advanced trench-gate field-stop technology, it offers low conduction and switching losses for high-efficiency power conversion.

The module uses direct bond copper (DBC) substrate for excellent thermal performance with junction-to-case thermal resistance of 0.45°C/W. The compact package is ideal for space-constrained industrial applications.
Applications include variable frequency drives, servo drives, solar inverters, and UPS systems requiring reliable 1200V switching capability.
features: Low Vce(sat) for reduced conduction losses
DBC substrate for excellent thermal performance
Low inductance design for reduced EMI

faeReview.author: Michael Chen
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Power Electronics
faeReview.content: The MIXA60PD1200TS is an excellent choice for 5-15kW motor drive applications. I've used this module in numerous VFD designs with excellent results. The 1.7V Vce(sat) is competitive with premium brands, and the switching characteristics are well-balanced for 4-8kHz operation. The built-in NTC is valuable for thermal monitoring and protection. I particularly appreciate the consistent quality - modules from different production batches show minimal parameter variation. For applications requiring higher current, the module can be paralleled with proper current sharing design. I recommend using 15V gate drive with -8V negative voltage for optimal switching performance.
faeReview.highlight: Reliable 1200V 60A module ideal for industrial motor drives and inverters

alternativeParts.reason: Lower current rating for reduced power applications

companionParts.description: Fast recovery diode for external freewheeling

faqs.question: What gate drive voltage is recommended for this module?
faqs.answer: Recommended gate drive: +15V turn-on, -8V turn-off. The 15V positive voltage ensures low Vce(sat) and fast turn-on. The -8V negative voltage prevents false turn-on from dV/dt during switching transitions. Use gate resistors of 10-22Ω to control switching speed and EMI. Gate drive power requirement is approximately 1W per IGBT at 8kHz switching frequency. Ensure gate driver can provide 2A peak current for fast switching.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use +15V/-8V gate drive with 10-22Ω gate resistors for optimal performance.

products.name: 1200V 100A IGBT Module
products.shortDescription: High-current dual IGBT module with 1200V rating and 100A continuous current for high-power motor drives.
descriptionParagraphs: The MIXA100PD1200TS delivers 1200V 100A capability in a compact dual module package. Advanced field-stop IGBT technology provides excellent conduction and switching characteristics for high-efficiency power conversion up to 30kW.

The module features optimized DBC substrate design with 0.28°C/W thermal resistance, enabling high current capability with manageable junction temperatures. Integrated temperature sensing and low-inductance layout simplify system design.
Ideal for high-power industrial drives, renewable energy inverters, and heavy-duty power supplies requiring reliable 1200V switching.

faeReview.author: David Liu
faeReview.title: Principal FAE - Industrial Power
faeReview.content: The MIXA100PD1200TS is my go-to module for high-power industrial applications. The 100A rating with excellent thermal performance makes it ideal for 20-30kW drives. I've successfully used these in crane drives, extruders, and large pump applications. The 1.85V Vce(sat) is very reasonable for this current level. Switching losses are manageable at 4-6kHz. The 62mm package provides good mounting stability and heat spreading. For thermal design, I recommend heatsinks with 0.15°C/W or better with forced air cooling. The module has proven very reliable - zero field failures in over 3 years of deployment.
faeReview.highlight: High-reliability 100A module for demanding industrial power applications

alternativeParts.reason: Lower current for reduced power requirements

faqs.question: What heatsink is required for 80A continuous operation?
faqs.answer: For 80A continuous operation: Conduction loss = 80A × 1.85V × 0.5 (duty) = 74W per IGBT. Switching loss at 6kHz ≈ 15W. Total ≈ 90W per IGBT. With Rth(j-c) = 0.28°C/W, Tj = Tc + 25°C. For Tj(max) = 150°C and Ta = 40°C, required Rth(h-a) = (150-40)/90 - 0.28 - 0.1 = 0.84°C/W. Recommend heatsink with 0.6-0.8°C/W with forced air (3-5 m/s). Use thermal interface material with <0.5°C·cm²/W thermal resistance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use heatsink with Rth < 0.8°C/W with forced air for 80A operation.

products.name: 1200V 150A IGBT Module
products.shortDescription: High-power dual IGBT module with 1200V rating and 150A continuous current for heavy industrial applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The MIXA150PD1200TS provides 1200V 150A capability for high-power industrial applications up to 45kW. State-of-the-art field-stop IGBT technology delivers industry-leading performance with low conduction and switching losses.

The 62mm package with advanced DBC substrate achieves 0.20°C/W thermal resistance, enabling high current handling with excellent reliability. The module is designed for demanding applications including large motor drives and renewable energy systems.
Suitable for high-power motor drives, grid-tie inverters, and industrial power conversion requiring maximum performance and reliability.

faeReview.author: Steven Park
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - High Power Systems
faeReview.content: The MIXA150PD1200TS is designed for the most demanding industrial applications. I've deployed these in large extruder drives (40kW), cement mill drives, and grid-scale solar inverters. The 150A rating with 0.20°C/W thermal resistance provides excellent power handling. While Vce(sat) is slightly higher at 1.95V, the overall efficiency remains excellent due to optimized chip design. The module requires careful thermal design - liquid cooling may be preferred for continuous 150A operation. Switching frequency should be limited to 3-5kHz to manage losses. This module represents IXYS's capability in high-power IGBT technology.
faeReview.highlight: Ultra-high current 150A module for heavy industrial power applications

faqs.question: Can this module be used for 150A continuous operation?
faqs.answer: 150A continuous operation requires careful thermal design: At 150A, conduction loss = 150A × 1.95V × 0.5 = 146W per IGBT. Switching loss at 4kHz ≈ 25W. Total ≈ 170W. With Rth(j-c) = 0.20°C/W and Rth(c-h) = 0.08°C/W, required Rth(h-a) = (150-40)/170 - 0.28 = 0.37°C/W. This requires liquid cooling or very large heatsink with high airflow. For air-cooled systems, recommend 100-120A continuous with appropriate heatsink. Always verify thermal design with actual measurements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use liquid cooling for 150A continuous. Limit to 100-120A for air-cooled systems.

products.name: 600V 75A IGBT Module
products.shortDescription: 600V dual IGBT module with 75A current rating for lower voltage motor drives and power supplies.
descriptionParagraphs: The MIXA75PD600TS offers 600V 75A capability optimized for 220-240V AC applications. The lower voltage rating provides improved conduction characteristics compared to 1200V devices, making it ideal for single-phase drives and appliances.

Featuring advanced trench-gate technology with Vce(sat) of only 1.45V, this module delivers excellent efficiency for cost-sensitive applications. The compact 34mm package simplifies mounting and thermal management.
Perfect for single-phase motor drives, appliance inverters, and lower voltage power supplies requiring reliable 600V switching.

faeReview.author: Michael Chen
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Power Electronics
faeReview.content: The MIXA75PD600TS is an excellent choice for 230V single-phase applications. The 1.45V Vce(sat) is outstanding - much better than 1200V devices. I've used these in HVAC drives, appliance inverters, and pump controllers with excellent efficiency results. The 600V rating provides adequate margin for 230V systems with PFC. The switching losses are low enough for 8-12kHz operation, enabling quiet motor operation. For thermal design, the module is quite manageable - even moderate heatsinks work well for 50-60A operation. This is a cost-effective alternative to 1200V modules for lower voltage applications.
faeReview.highlight: Cost-effective 600V module with excellent efficiency for 230V applications

faqs.question: When should I choose 600V vs 1200V modules?
faqs.answer: Choose 600V modules for: (1) 220-240V AC single-phase systems, (2) 300-400V DC bus applications, (3) Cost-sensitive designs where 1200V is overkill. The lower voltage devices have better conduction characteristics (lower Vce(sat)) and are less expensive. Choose 1200V for: (1) 380-480V three-phase systems, (2) 600-700V DC bus, (3) Applications requiring voltage margin for transients. The 2x voltage margin rule applies: for 400V DC bus, use 800V+ devices, so 1200V is appropriate.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use 600V for 230V systems. Use 1200V for 380-480V systems.

categories.name: Power Diodes
categories.description: IXYS power diodes include fast recovery, ultra-fast, and standard recovery types for rectification, freewheeling, and snubber applications.
categories.longDescription: IXYS power diodes provide reliable rectification and protection for power electronics applications. The portfolio includes fast recovery diodes (25-100ns) for high-frequency SMPS, ultra-fast diodes (<25ns) for critical snubber applications, and standard recovery diodes for line-frequency rectification. Available in voltage ratings from 100V to 2000V and current ratings from 1A to 100A, IXYS diodes serve diverse applications including output rectification, freewheeling protection, and snubber circuits. All devices feature soft recovery characteristics to minimize EMI and voltage overshoots.

selectionGuide.title: IXYS Power Diode Selection Guide
selectionGuide.description: Comprehensive guide to selecting the right IXYS diode for your application

series.name: DSEI Series
series.description: Fast recovery diodes for high-frequency applications

series.name: DSEP Series
series.description: Ultra-fast recovery diodes for critical snubber applications

faqs.question: How do I select the right reverse recovery time?
faqs.answer: Reverse recovery time selection: (1) <25ns (ultra-fast) - for snubber circuits, high-frequency DC-DC converters (>100kHz), and critical applications where switching losses dominate; (2) 25-50ns (fast) - for SMPS output rectification, motor drive freewheeling, and general high-frequency applications (50-100kHz); (3) 50-100ns (standard fast) - for PFC circuits, lower frequency SMPS (20-50kHz), and cost-sensitive designs; (4) >100ns (standard) - for line-frequency rectification (50/60Hz) where switching losses are negligible. Faster recovery diodes have higher forward voltage drop, so balance switching vs conduction losses for your application.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use ultra-fast (<25ns) for snubbers, fast (25-50ns) for SMPS, standard fast (50-100ns) for PFC.

faqs.question: What is the difference between soft and snappy recovery?
faqs.answer: Recovery characteristics comparison: (1) Soft recovery - gradual decrease in reverse current produces smooth waveform with minimal overshoot. Lower EMI and reduced voltage spikes. Preferred for most applications. (2) Snappy recovery - abrupt termination of reverse current can cause voltage overshoots and ringing. Higher EMI. May require additional snubber circuits. (3) IXYS diodes feature controlled soft recovery optimized for minimal losses and EMI. (4) Selection - always prefer soft recovery for high-frequency applications. Snappy recovery may be acceptable for low-frequency line rectifiers. (5) Testing - verify recovery characteristics under actual operating conditions including temperature and current.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose soft recovery diodes for high-frequency applications to minimize EMI and voltage spikes.

products.name: 600V 60A Fast Recovery Diode
products.shortDescription: Fast recovery diode with 600V rating, 60A current, and 35ns reverse recovery time for SMPS and motor drives.
descriptionParagraphs: The DSEI60-06A is a high-performance fast recovery diode featuring 600V reverse voltage capability and 60A average forward current. With 35ns reverse recovery time and soft recovery characteristics, it minimizes switching losses and EMI in high-frequency applications.

The TO-247 package provides excellent thermal performance with low junction-to-case thermal resistance. The diode's 1.3V forward voltage drop at rated current ensures efficient conduction with minimal power loss.
Ideal for output rectification in SMPS, freewheeling protection in motor drives, and PFC circuits requiring fast switching with low losses.
features: TO-247 package with excellent thermal performance

faeReview.author: Michael Chen
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Power Electronics
faeReview.content: The DSEI60-06A is my standard recommendation for 600V fast recovery applications. The 35ns trr is fast enough for most SMPS up to 100kHz, and the soft recovery minimizes EMI issues. I've used these in hundreds of designs - PFC circuits, motor drive freewheeling, and SMPS outputs. The 1.3V Vf is reasonable for a fast diode. The TO-247 package handles the 60A rating well with proper heatsinking. For thermal design, at 40A continuous with 50% duty cycle, conduction loss is 26W - manageable with a small heatsink. The avalanche rating is a nice bonus for ruggedness. Overall, an excellent general-purpose fast recovery diode.
faeReview.highlight: Versatile 600V fast recovery diode ideal for SMPS and motor drive applications

alternativeParts.reason: Lower current rating for reduced power applications

faqs.question: What is the maximum operating frequency for this diode?
faqs.answer: The DSEI60-06A with 35ns trr is suitable for switching frequencies up to 100kHz in hard-switching applications. At 100kHz, switching losses become significant - approximately 10-15W depending on current and voltage. For frequencies above 100kHz, consider ultra-fast diodes with trr <25ns. The soft recovery characteristic helps minimize EMI at high frequencies. For PFC applications at 65kHz, this diode provides excellent performance with good efficiency.
faqs.decisionGuide: Suitable for applications up to 100kHz. Use ultra-fast diodes for higher frequencies.

products.name: 600V 120A Fast Recovery Diode
products.shortDescription: High-current fast recovery diode with 600V rating and 120A capability for high-power SMPS and inverters.
descriptionParagraphs: The DSEI120-06A delivers 600V 120A capability with 40ns reverse recovery time for high-power applications. The ultra-low forward voltage drop of 1.25V at rated current maximizes efficiency in high-current rectification.
The TO-247 package with advanced thermal design achieves 0.45°C/W junction-to-case thermal resistance, enabling high current handling with manageable junction temperatures.
Designed for high-power SMPS output rectification, welding equipment, and large motor drive freewheeling applications.

faeReview.author: David Liu
faeReview.title: Principal FAE - Power Systems
faeReview.content: The DSEI120-06A is the go-to diode for high-current 600V applications. The 120A rating with 1.25V Vf provides excellent efficiency. I've used these in 10kW+ SMPS, large welding power supplies, and high-power motor drives. The 40ns trr is still quite fast for this current level. Thermal design is critical - at 80A continuous, conduction loss is 100W, requiring substantial heatsinking. The soft recovery helps keep EMI manageable despite the high current. For very high current applications, these can be paralleled with careful layout. Overall, an excellent high-current fast recovery diode with proven reliability.
faeReview.highlight: High-current 120A fast recovery diode for demanding power applications

faqs.question: Can this diode be paralleled for higher current?
faqs.answer: Yes, the DSEI120-06A can be paralleled for currents above 120A: (1) Static sharing - matched Vf characteristics ensure current sharing at DC. Use diodes from same production lot. (2) Dynamic sharing - similar reverse recovery characteristics prevent current hogging during switching. (3) Layout - symmetric layout with equal trace lengths and impedances. (4) Thermal coupling - mount on common heatsink so temperature rise balances currents. (5) Derating - design for 10% current imbalance. Two diodes can reliably carry 200-220A. (6) Testing - verify current sharing with current probes at full load.
faqs.decisionGuide: Can parallel with proper layout and thermal coupling for 200A+ applications.

products.name: 1200V 60A Ultra-Fast Recovery Diode
products.shortDescription: Ultra-fast recovery diode with 1200V rating, 60A current, and 25ns reverse recovery time for high-voltage snubber applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The DSEP60-12A is an ultra-fast recovery diode featuring 1200V reverse voltage and 25ns reverse recovery time. Designed for high-voltage, high-frequency applications where minimal switching losses are critical.

The ultra-fast recovery with soft switching characteristics minimizes voltage overshoots and EMI in high-voltage circuits. The 1.4V forward voltage drop provides good efficiency despite the ultra-fast recovery.
Ideal for snubber circuits, high-voltage PFC, and critical high-frequency rectification applications requiring 1200V capability.
features: Low Qrr for reduced switching losses

faeReview.author: Steven Park
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - High Voltage Applications
faeReview.content: The DSEP60-12A is my choice for high-voltage ultra-fast applications. The 25ns trr with ultra-soft recovery is excellent for snubbers and high-voltage PFC. I've used these in 380V PFC circuits, solar inverters, and high-voltage SMPS. The 1200V rating provides good margin for 380-480V systems. While Vf is slightly higher than standard fast diodes, the reduced switching losses more than compensate in high-frequency applications. The soft recovery really helps minimize voltage spikes in inductive circuits. For snubber applications, the low Qrr (65nC) minimizes energy loss. A premium diode for demanding high-voltage applications.
faeReview.highlight: Ultra-fast 1200V diode ideal for high-voltage snubbers and PFC applications

faqs.question: When should I use ultra-fast vs fast recovery diodes?
faqs.answer: Ultra-fast diodes (trr <25ns) are preferred for: (1) High-voltage snubber circuits where low Qrr minimizes energy loss, (2) High-frequency applications (>100kHz) where switching losses dominate, (3) Critical applications where EMI must be minimized, (4) PFC circuits at high switching frequencies. Fast diodes (25-50ns) are sufficient for: (1) Standard SMPS up to 100kHz, (2) Motor drive freewheeling, (3) Cost-sensitive designs. The trade-off is that ultra-fast diodes typically have higher Vf and cost more. Use ultra-fast only when the application truly benefits from the faster recovery.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use ultra-fast for snubbers and >100kHz applications. Use fast for standard SMPS.

products.name: 1000V 30A Fast Recovery Diode
products.shortDescription: 1000V fast recovery diode with 30A rating for PFC circuits and high-voltage rectification.
descriptionParagraphs: The DSEI30-10A provides 1000V reverse voltage capability with 30A average current and 30ns reverse recovery time. The balanced performance makes it ideal for PFC applications and high-voltage rectification where both voltage margin and switching speed are important.
The TO-220 package offers cost-effective thermal management for medium power applications. The 1.35V forward voltage drop provides good efficiency for this voltage rating.
Suitable for PFC boost diodes, high-voltage SMPS, and motor drive applications requiring 1000V capability.

faeReview.author: Michael Chen
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Power Electronics
faeReview.content: The DSEI30-10A is a cost-effective solution for 1000V applications. The 30A rating with 30ns trr hits a sweet spot for PFC circuits and medium-power SMPS. I use these frequently in universal input PFC boost stages where 1000V provides margin for 380-400V output. The TO-220 package is easier to mount and heatsink than TO-247 for lower currents. At 20A continuous, losses are manageable with modest heatsinking. The 30ns recovery is fast enough for 65kHz PFC operation with reasonable efficiency. For the price, this is an excellent value diode for 1000V applications.
faeReview.highlight: Cost-effective 1000V fast recovery diode for PFC and SMPS applications

faqs.question: Is this diode suitable for PFC applications?
faqs.answer: Yes, the DSEI30-10A is excellent for PFC boost applications: (1) 1000V rating provides margin for 380-400V PFC output, (2) 30ns trr is fast enough for 65kHz operation with reasonable losses, (3) 30A rating handles up to 2-3kW PFC with single diode, (4) Soft recovery minimizes EMI and voltage spikes, (5) TO-220 package is cost-effective for PFC designs. For higher power PFC (>3kW), use DSEI60-06A or parallel two DSEI30-10A. The diode's characteristics are well-suited for continuous conduction mode (CCM) PFC where recovery losses are significant.
faqs.decisionGuide: Excellent for PFC up to 3kW. Use larger diodes or parallel for higher power.

categories.name: Thyristors
categories.description: IXYS thyristors include silicon controlled rectifiers (SCRs) and triacs for phase control and AC power switching applications.
categories.longDescription: IXYS thyristors provide reliable AC power control for industrial, commercial, and consumer applications. The portfolio includes standard SCRs for rectification and phase control, sensitive gate SCRs for trigger applications, and triacs for AC switching. Available in voltage ratings from 400V to 1600V and current ratings from 1A to 100A, IXYS thyristors serve diverse applications including motor speed control, heating systems, lighting dimmers, and power supplies. All devices feature high surge current capability and excellent dv/dt performance for reliable operation in demanding environments.

selectionGuide.title: IXYS Thyristor Selection Guide
selectionGuide.description: Comprehensive guide to selecting the right IXYS thyristor for your application

series.name: CS Series
series.description: Standard SCRs for phase control applications

faqs.question: What is the difference between SCRs and triacs?
faqs.answer: SCR (Silicon Controlled Rectifier) vs Triac: (1) SCR - unidirectional device, conducts current in one direction only. Used for DC control and half-wave AC control. Higher voltage and current ratings available. (2) Triac - bidirectional device, conducts in both directions. Used for full-wave AC control. Simplifies circuit design for AC applications. (3) Gate sensitivity - triacs generally require higher gate trigger current. (4) Commutation - triacs have commutation limitations at high frequencies. (5) Applications - use SCRs for DC motor control, battery chargers, half-wave circuits. Use triacs for AC motor speed control, lighting dimmers, heater control. (6) Cost - SCRs are generally less expensive than triacs of similar ratings.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use SCRs for DC and half-wave AC control. Use triacs for full-wave AC control.

faqs.question: How do I protect thyristors from dv/dt and di/dt?
faqs.answer: Thyristor protection techniques: (1) dv/dt protection - snubber circuit (RC network) across thyristor limits voltage rise rate. Typical values: 100-470Ω resistor, 0.1-0.47μF capacitor. (2) di/dt protection - series inductor limits current rise rate during turn-on. Typical: 10-100μH. (3) Overvoltage - TVS diode or MOV across device. (4) Overcurrent - fast-acting fuse in series. (5) Gate protection - series resistor limits gate current, parallel resistor prevents false triggering. (6) Heat management - adequate heatsinking for current rating. (7) Isolation - proper creepage and clearance for voltage rating. Proper protection ensures reliable operation and long device life.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use RC snubbers for dv/dt, series inductors for di/dt, and proper heatsinking.

products.name: 1600V 45A Standard SCR
products.shortDescription: High-voltage standard SCR with 1600V rating and 45A current for phase control and rectification applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The CS45-16IO1 is a high-performance standard SCR featuring 1600V blocking voltage and 45A RMS current capability. The sensitive gate allows triggering with low gate current, simplifying drive circuit design.

The TO-247AD package provides excellent thermal performance with 0.9°C/W junction-to-case thermal resistance. High surge current capability (600A) ensures reliable operation under overload conditions.
Ideal for phase control applications, motor speed control, heating systems, and battery chargers requiring high-voltage capability.

faeReview.author: Michael Chen
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Power Electronics
faeReview.content: The CS45-16IO1 is a versatile high-voltage SCR for phase control applications. The 1600V rating handles 480V AC systems with plenty of margin. The 45A rating is good for up to 15-20kW single-phase control. I use these in heater controllers, battery chargers, and DC motor drives. The sensitive gate (30mA) works well with optocoupler drives. Surge capability is excellent - I've seen these survive repeated inrush currents in heater applications. For thermal design, at 30A with conduction angle 90°, dissipation is about 40W - manageable with moderate heatsinking. A reliable workhorse SCR for industrial phase control.
faeReview.highlight: Reliable 1600V 45A SCR for industrial phase control applications

faqs.question: What gate drive circuit is recommended for this SCR?
faqs.answer: Recommended gate drive: (1) Gate current - provide 2-3x minimum trigger current (60-90mA) for reliable triggering. (2) Gate voltage - 1.5-3V typical when conducting. (3) Pulse width - minimum 10μs, longer for inductive loads (100-500μs). (4) Drive circuit - use optocoupler for isolation (MOC3052 series), transistor buffer for higher current. (5) Protection - 100Ω series resistor limits gate current, protect against reverse gate voltage. (6) Trigger timing - synchronize with AC zero crossing for full-wave control. (7) dv/dt - ensure dv/dt at turn-off is within rating to prevent false triggering.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use optocoupler drive with 60-90mA gate current and 100-500μs pulse width.

products.name: 1600V 60A Standard SCR
products.shortDescription: High-current SCR with 1600V rating and 60A capability for high-power phase control applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The CS60-16IO1 delivers 1600V 60A capability for high-power phase control and rectification. The standard gate sensitivity provides reliable triggering with simple drive circuits.
The TO-247AD package with enhanced thermal design achieves 0.75°C/W thermal resistance, enabling high current handling with manageable junction temperatures.
Designed for high-power heating control, large battery chargers, and industrial motor drives requiring 1600V blocking capability.

faeReview.author: David Liu
faeReview.title: Principal FAE - Industrial Power
faeReview.content: The CS60-16IO1 is my choice for high-power phase control. The 60A rating handles up to 25-30kW single-phase loads. I've deployed these in large industrial ovens, electroplating rectifiers, and DC motor controls. The 1600V rating provides excellent margin for 480V systems. Gate drive is straightforward - 50mA trigger current works well with standard optocouplers. Surge capability (800A) handles transformer inrush and heater cold starts. Thermal design at 50A is manageable with good heatsinking. The device has proven very reliable in harsh industrial environments. For higher currents, these parallel reasonably well with matched devices.
faeReview.highlight: High-current 60A SCR for demanding industrial phase control

faqs.question: What is the maximum power this SCR can control?
faqs.answer: Maximum power depends on voltage and conduction angle: (1) 240V AC single-phase - up to 15kW at 60A with full conduction, (2) 480V AC single-phase - up to 30kW at 60A, (3) Three-phase - up to 50kW in 3-phase full-wave configuration, (4) Conduction angle - power reduces proportionally with phase control (50% power at 90° conduction), (5) Thermal limit - actual continuous current may be limited by heatsinking. For resistive loads (heaters), use 50-60A continuous. For motor loads, 40-50A is more practical due to higher losses. Always verify thermal design for specific application conditions.
faqs.decisionGuide: Up to 15kW at 240V, 30kW at 480V single-phase. Verify thermal design.

products.name: 1600V 25A Triac
products.shortDescription: High-voltage triac with 1600V rating and 25A current for AC switching and phase control applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The CT25-16 is a high-performance triac featuring 1600V blocking voltage and 25A RMS current capability. The sensitive gate allows triggering in all four quadrants with low gate current.
The TO-220 package provides cost-effective thermal management for medium power AC switching applications. High surge current capability (250A) ensures reliable operation under overload conditions.
Ideal for AC motor speed control, lighting dimmers, heater control, and appliance switching requiring 1600V capability.

faeReview.author: Michael Chen
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Power Electronics
faeReview.content: The CT25-16 is a versatile triac for AC switching applications. The 1600V rating is excellent for 240V systems with high line voltage or transients. The 25A rating handles up to 6kW resistive loads. I use these in heater controllers, fan speed controls, and lighting dimmers. Four-quadrant triggering is convenient - works with positive or negative gate drive. The TO-220 package is easy to mount and heatsink. At 20A continuous, dissipation is about 30W - manageable with modest heatsinking. Commutation is reliable at 50-60Hz. For the price, this is an excellent general-purpose triac for AC control applications.
faeReview.highlight: Versatile 1600V triac for AC switching and phase control

faqs.question: What is the difference between standard and snubberless triacs?
faqs.answer: Standard vs snubberless triacs: (1) Standard triacs - require RC snubber circuit for inductive loads to prevent false triggering from dv/dt at commutation. (2) Snubberless triacs - have higher dv/dt capability (2000V/μs vs 500V/μs), can handle inductive loads without external snubber. (3) Applications - use standard triacs for resistive loads or with snubber. Use snubberless for inductive loads (motors, transformers) to simplify circuit. (4) Cost - snubberless triacs are more expensive. (5) CT25-16 is standard type - add snubber (100Ω + 0.1μF) for inductive loads. For motor control, consider snubberless types or ensure proper snubber design.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use standard triacs with snubber for inductive loads. Snubberless types simplify motor control design.

products.name: 1600V 40A Triac
products.shortDescription: High-current triac with 1600V rating and 40A capability for high-power AC switching applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The CT40-16 delivers 1600V 40A capability for high-power AC switching and phase control. The standard gate sensitivity provides reliable triggering with simple drive circuits.

The TO-220 package with enhanced thermal design handles higher currents while maintaining cost-effectiveness. High surge current capability (400A) ensures reliable operation under overload conditions.
Designed for high-power heater control, large motor speed control, and industrial AC switching requiring 1600V blocking capability.

faeReview.author: David Liu
faeReview.title: Principal FAE - Industrial Power
faeReview.content: The CT40-16 is my choice for high-power AC switching. The 40A rating handles up to 10kW at 240V - excellent for large heaters and industrial equipment. The 1600V rating provides good margin for industrial environments. I've used these in commercial oven controls, large fan speed controllers, and industrial lighting systems. Gate drive is straightforward with standard optocouplers. At 35A continuous, thermal management is important - use good heatsinking. The device commutes reliably with proper snubber design for inductive loads. For resistive heating loads, this triac provides excellent value and reliability.
faeReview.highlight: High-current 40A triac for industrial AC power control

faqs.question: Can this triac be used for motor speed control?
faqs.answer: Yes, CT40-16 can be used for AC motor speed control with proper design: (1) Universal motors - work well with phase control, speed varies with voltage. (2) Induction motors - phase control provides limited speed range (typically 2:1), may cause overheating at low speeds. (3) Snubber required - use RC snubber (47-100Ω + 0.1-0.22μF) across triac for inductive loads. (4) dv/dt - motor inductance can cause commutation problems, snubber helps. (5) Filtering - motor may require output filter to reduce noise and heating. (6) Thermal - motors have high starting current, ensure adequate heatsinking. (7) Applications - best for fan/pump loads with quadratic torque. Not recommended for constant torque loads requiring wide speed range.
faqs.decisionGuide: Suitable for universal motors and fan/pump loads. Use snubber for all motor applications.
